operations.value: Short sleep studies using 3 g near bedtime did not identify a strong next-day sedative signal, but they were too small and brief to establish long-term safety; gastrointestinal discomfort can occur with amino-acid supplements, and people who are pregnant, have significant kidney or liver disease, or take prescription medicines should seek clinical guidance before regular high-dose use.
operations.notes: Combines the limited short-term sleep-study safety signal with an explicit limitation on long-term certainty and prudent higher-risk-group guidance.

sources.title: The effect of glycine administration on the characteristics of physiological systems in human adults: A systematic review
The Effects of Glycine on Subjective Daytime Performance in Partially Sleep-Restricted Healthy Volunteers

notes: Adds a conservative safety statement without claiming comprehensive long-term safety.
